main_dirt.bias_notes: A 1 1/8-mile oval notorious for being 'The Conveyor Belt'. The surface is hard, fast, and sandy, producing severe kickback. It frequently plays with a massive 'GOLDEN RAIL' bias. Pure early speed on the inside is incredibly dominant, especially in 6F sprints.
main_dirt.post_bias: Inside posts are gold in sprints IF the horse has the speed to clear. However, in the unique One-Turn Mile (run out of a chute), Post 1 is a severe disadvantage because horses get pinned inside; Posts 3-6 are ideal.

main_turf.bias_notes: A fast, flat turf course that requires tactical speed. Deep closers can win if the pace melts down, but 'ground-saving' trips hugging the rail are generally required to hit the board.
main_turf.post_bias: Inside to middle posts are preferred. Outside posts (8+) in two-turn turf routes (1 1/16 miles) are a massive disadvantage due to the extremely short run into the first turn.

tapeta.bias_notes: The inner synthetic track provides a perfectly fair, forgiving surface. Unlike the main dirt track, speed is highly vulnerable here. The Tapeta heavily rewards patient stalkers and deep closers ('Swoopers').
tapeta.post_bias: Very fair across the board. Outside posts are not heavily penalized as riders have plenty of time to find cover.

handicapping_angles.the_golden_rail_wire: In dirt sprints, heavily upgrade 'Need-the-Lead' (E) types drawn inside. If they miss the break, they lose, but if they clear early, they are nearly impossible to run down.
handicapping_angles.kickback_elimination: Because the kickback on the dirt is so severe, aggressively downgrade stalkers drawn inside (Posts 1-3) who lack early speed. They will get buried in flying sand and frequently stop running.
handicapping_angles.the_championship_meet_shift: Gulfstream is dynamically tiered. During the Winter Championship Meet (Dec-Mar), it is an elite Tier 1 track. During the Summer (Apr-Nov), it drops to a Tier 2 track. Adjust shipper class penalties heavily based on the season.
handicapping_angles.tapeta_turf_translators: When turf races are rained off onto the Tapeta, heavily upgrade horses with proven turf form over those with only dirt form. Grass mechanics translate beautifully to the synthetic.
